0.q: PdbERF109-OE lines exhibited a significant enhancement in the activities of antioxidant enzymes... while the levels of malondialdehyde (MDA) and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) were markedly reduced... indicating significantly enhanced reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging capacity

1.q: overexpression of PdbWRKY31 enhanced peroxidase (POD) and catalase (CAT) activities, reduced hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) accumulation, and cell damage induced by salt stress.

2.q: the overexpression of PdbbZIP61 can reduce cell damage by increasing ROS scavenging.

3.q: PdbCRF6 could interact with PdbCRF5 to coregulate the expression of PdbbZIP61, and the OE of PdbCRF6 also reduced the salt tolerance of Shanxin poplar.

4.q: PdbWRKY31 enhanced salt tolerance in Shanxin poplar by binding to the W-box element on the promoter of the target genes (PdbCAT1 and 3) and promoting their expression.

5.q: PdbCRF5 could regulate the expression of PdbbZIP61 by binding to ABRE motif... PdbCRF5 negatively regulates the salt tolerance of Shanxin poplar by inhibiting the PdbbZIP61.

6.q: overexpression of PdbbZIP61 can improve the salt tolerance of Shanxin poplar by regulating the expression of the PdbNCED genes to increase the ABA content.

7.q: ChIP-PCR and qRT-PCR assays showed that PdbWRKY31 increased PdbCAT1 and PdbCAT3 expression by binding to the W-box element on their promoters.

8.q: ChIP-PCR and qRT-PCR assays showed that PdbWRKY31 increased PdbCAT1 and PdbCAT3 expression by binding to the W-box element on their promoters.

9.q: the overexpression of PdbbZIP61 can reduce cell damage by increasing ROS scavenging.

10.q: PdbWRKY31 enhanced salt tolerance in Shanxin poplar by binding to the W-box element on the promoter of the target genes (PdbCAT1 and 3) and promoting their expression.

11.q: ChIP‒PCR, EMSA, and Y1H confirmed that PdbCRF5 can regulate the expression of the PdbbZIP61 by binding to ABRE element.
